_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn how diverse worldviews can contribute to addressing social and environmental challenges.

_source.w: We learn how diverse worldviews can contribute to addressing social and environmental challenges.
y: Complex global challenges require diverse perspectives — no single worldview has all the answers.
Indigenous and non-Western worldviews offer insights that complement and challenge dominant approaches.
Learning to draw on diverse worldviews develops our ability to think creatively about global problems.
eg: We investigate how Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander knowledge systems contribute to environmental management.
We explore how different cultural perspectives approach challenges like climate change, poverty and conflict.
We analyse how diverse worldviews bring different strengths to problem-solving — different values, priorities and methods.
We discuss how integrating Indigenous knowledge with Western science produces better environmental outcomes.
We investigate how intercultural dialogue has contributed to peace-building and conflict resolution.
We evaluate how our own worldview might be both helpful and limiting when addressing complex challenges.
